title: GripEventDetails
description: Per-stage organisational details for one stage of a motorsport event, transmitted from event control to in-car devices — start line, finish line, speed limits along the liaison/transit, staging areas (parc fermé, pre-stage holds), time controls, free-text transit instructions, discrete course markers (e.g. rallyX cones with pass-side), and continuous course boundaries (e.g. paved racetrack edges or cone-free rallyX boundary tape). Distinct from stage-geometry (the physical shape of the timed stage) and pace-note (driver shorthand). References its parent event by natural key.

extensions.description: App-specific namespaced data. Keys are app slugs (kebab-case recommended); values are objects owned by that app.

geoPoint.description: WGS84 geographic position. Latitude in degrees [-90, 90], longitude in degrees [-180, 180]. Altitude in metres above the ellipsoid; null when not known.

controlLine.description: A geographic line crossing the road, defined by two end points (typically the two sides of the road surface) plus optional free-text description. Used for startLine / finishLine and for any user-defined crossings consumers (like stage-geometry) may want to reference.

speedLimit.description: A speed limit applying from `start` to `end` along the route. Use multiple speedLimit entries for sections with different limits.

stagingArea.description: A geographic area where cars gather (pre-stage hold, parc fermé, service in/out, regroup, refuel).

name.description: Crew-facing label, e.g. 'Pre-stage SS1' or 'Service A'.

timeControl.description: A time-control point — crews must arrive within their target window. `targetTime` is null for 'as-you-arrive' controls.

targetTime.description: ISO 8601 UTC target arrival time. Null for 'as-arrived' or unscheduled controls.

transitInstruction.description: A turn-by-turn instruction for the liaison/transit before or after the timed stage. Free-text with an optional geographic anchor and optional cumulative distance.

distanceMeters.description: Optional cumulative distance from the start of this transit leg, in metres.

courseMarker.description: A physical marker along the course (cone, gate post, flag, etc.) with an optional pass-side rule. For paired markers that the car must drive between (e.g. a gate), use a `controlLine` instead.

passSide.description: Which side of the marker the car must pass on, from the driver's perspective. Omit when the marker is purely informational and carries no pass-side constraint.

courseBoundary.description: A continuous course boundary expressed as an ordered polyline of geoPoints. Use for paved-circuit track edges, cone-free rallyX boundary tape, or any other line-defined limit of the drivable area. Multiple boundary objects are normal — one per edge (e.g. left and right), or one per disjoint segment.

name.description: Optional crew-facing label, e.g. 'Outer track edge' or 'Right boundary'.

side.description: Which side of the course this boundary defines. left/right are relative to the driver's forward direction (typical for point-to-point courses); inner/outer are relative to a closed lap (typical for racetracks).

points.description: Ordered polyline of geoPoints defining the boundary.

closed.description: True if the boundary forms a closed loop (the implicit segment from the last point back to the first is part of the boundary). Default false.

schemaVersion.description: Version of the event-details schema this document conforms to.

event.description: Natural-key reference to the parent event header.

courseMarkers.description: Ordered list of physical markers along the course. Array order is the sequence in which they are encountered.

courseBoundaries.description: Continuous polyline boundaries that delimit the drivable area. One entry per distinct boundary (typically left + right for point-to-point; inner + outer for closed loops).
